UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile information and application-specific settings, excluding authentication details.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

firstName.description: The first name of the user.

lastName.description: The last name of the user.

email.description: The email address of the user, used for communication.

role.description: The role of the user within the application (e.g., 'engineer', 'data_consumer'). This dictates UI views and permissions.

preferences.description: User-specific settings and preferences, such as UI theme (e.g., dark mode) or notification settings, stored as a JSON string.

Workspace.title: Workspace
Workspace.description: Organizes pipelines and facilitates collaboration among multiple users.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Workspace entity.

description.description: A brief description of the workspace's purpose.

ownerId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who owns this workspace. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Workspace)

memberIds.description: References to UserProfiles who are members of this workspace and can collaborate. (Relationship: UserProfile N:N Workspace)

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the workspace was created.

updatedAt.description: The timestamp when the workspace was last updated.

Pipeline.title: Pipeline
Pipeline.description: Represents a logical data pipeline, acting as a container for its versions.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Pipeline entity.

name.description: The name of the data pipeline.

description.description: A description of the data pipeline's purpose and functionality.

workspaceId.description: Reference to the Workspace this pipeline belongs to. (Relationship: Workspace 1:N Pipeline)

ownerId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who owns this pipeline. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Pipeline)

currentPipelineVersionId.description: Reference to the currently active or production version of this pipeline. (Relationship: Pipeline 1:1 PipelineVersion)

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the pipeline was created.

updatedAt.description: The timestamp when the pipeline was last updated.

PipelineVersion.title: PipelineVersion
PipelineVersion.description: Represents a specific historical or current version of a data pipeline, supporting lineage versioning.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PipelineVersion entity.

pipelineId.description: Reference to the Pipeline this version belongs to. (Relationship: Pipeline 1:N PipelineVersion)

versionNumber.description: The sequential version number for this pipeline's evolution.

status.description: The status of this pipeline version (e.g., 'draft', 'published', 'archived', 'production').

createdAt.description: The timestamp when this pipeline version was created.

createdByUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who created this pipeline version. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N PipelineVersion)

Node.title: Node
Node.description: Represents a single component within a specific version of a data pipeline, such as a source, transformation, or destination.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Node entity.

pipelineVersionId.description: Reference to the PipelineVersion this node belongs to. (Relationship: PipelineVersion 1:N Node)

name.description: The display name of the node on the canvas.

description.description: A description of the node's function.

configuration.description: A JSON string containing the specific configuration parameters for this node type (e.g., database connection details for a source, transformation rules for a transform).

xPosition.description: The X-coordinate position of the node on the canvas for visualization.

yPosition.description: The Y-coordinate position of the node on the canvas for visualization.

status.description: The current operational status of the node (e.g., 'running', 'stopped', 'error', 'pending').

dataQualityMetrics.description: A JSON string containing data quality metrics associated with this node (e.g., 'completeness', 'validity', 'freshness').

catalogTransformationId.description: Optional reference to a TransformationCatalog entry if this node represents a pre-defined transformation. (Relationship: TransformationCatalog 1:N Node)

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the node was created.

updatedAt.description: The timestamp when the node was last updated.

Connection.title: Connection
Connection.description: Represents a directed link between two nodes within a specific version of a data pipeline, illustrating data flow.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Connection entity.

pipelineVersionId.description: Reference to the PipelineVersion this connection belongs to. (Relationship: PipelineVersion 1:N Connection)

sourceNodeId.description: Reference to the source Node in this connection. (Relationship: Node 1:N Connection)

destinationNodeId.description: Reference to the destination Node in this connection. (Relationship: Node 1:N Connection)

sourcePort.description: Optional identifier for the specific output port of the source node.

destinationPort.description: Optional identifier for the specific input port of the destination node.

description.description: A description of the data flow or purpose of this connection.

TransformationCatalog.title: TransformationCatalog
TransformationCatalog.description: A catalogue of pre-defined data transformations that users can select and configure in their pipelines.

id.description: Unique identifier for the TransformationCatalog entity.

name.description: The display name of the pre-defined transformation.

description.description: A detailed description of what the transformation does.

parametersSchema.description: A JSON string representing a JSON Schema that defines the expected configuration parameters for this transformation.

iconUrl.description: URL to an icon representing this transformation in the catalogue.

definition.description: Stores user-specific profile information and application preferences (e.g., dark mode setting). This collection is private, accessible only by the authenticated user matching `{userId}`. It is created during the user onboarding process.

Top-level collection for collaborative workspaces. Documents include `ownerId` and a denormalized `members` map (e.g., `{uid1: 'member', uid2: 'admin'}`) for direct authorization checks by members or the owner. This map is derived from the `memberIds` array provided in the schema.

params.name: workspaceId
params.description: The unique identifier of the workspace.

definition.description: Contains logical data pipelines within a workspace. Each document includes denormalized `workspaceOwnerId`, `workspaceMembers` (map), and `pipelineOwnerId` for authorization independence, eliminating `get()` calls to parent documents in security rules.

params.name: workspaceId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent workspace.

params.name: pipelineId
params.description: The unique identifier of the pipeline.

definition.description: Stores specific versions of a pipeline, supporting lineage. Each document includes denormalized `workspaceOwnerId`, `workspaceMembers` (map), `pipelineOwnerId`, and `pipelineVersionCreatorId` for authorization independence.

params.name: workspaceId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent workspace.

params.name: pipelineId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent pipeline.

params.name: versionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the pipeline version.

definition.description: Represents components within a pipeline version (source, transformation, destination). Each document includes denormalized `workspaceOwnerId`, `workspaceMembers` (map), `pipelineOwnerId`, and `pipelineVersionCreatorId` for authorization independence.

params.name: workspaceId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent workspace.

params.name: pipelineId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent pipeline.

params.name: versionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent pipeline version.

params.name: nodeId
params.description: The unique identifier of the node.

definition.description: Represents directed links between nodes within a pipeline version. Each document includes denormalized `workspaceOwnerId`, `workspaceMembers` (map), `pipelineOwnerId`, and `pipelineVersionCreatorId` for authorization independence.

params.name: workspaceId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent workspace.

params.name: pipelineId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent pipeline.

params.name: versionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent pipeline version.

params.name: connectionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the connection.

definition.description: A global, read-only collection of pre-defined data transformations, accessible by all authenticated users.

params.name: transformationId
params.description: The unique identifier of the transformation in the catalog.

definition.description: Publicly accessible, read-only pipelines for demonstration purposes. Documents in this collection are a snapshot of a `PipelineVersion` and will embed their associated `nodes` (as an array of `Node` objects) and `connections` (as an array of `Connection` objects) for self-contained access and simple security rules. This ensures a single `get()` operation retrieves the complete demo pipeline structure.

params.name: demoPipelineId
params.description: The unique identifier of the demo pipeline.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to adhere strictly to the core design principles, prioritizing Authorization Independence, Clarity of Intent, DBAC, QAPs, and Invariants. Authorization Independence is achieved through strategic denormalization: for collaborative entities like Workspaces and their nested Pipelines, PipelineVersions, Nodes, and Connections, the `ownerId` and a `members` map (derived from the `memberIds` array for efficient lookup) from the parent Workspace are denormalized into all child documents. This eliminates the need for `get()` calls in security rules, making them atomic, simpler, and more performant, thus directly addressing Authorization Independence and supporting batch/transactional operations.

QAPs (Query as a Predicate) are supported primarily through Structural Segregation and the denormalized authorization fields. By organizing data into distinct collections with homogeneous security postures, such as `/users/{userId}/profile` for private data, `/workspaces/{workspaceId}` for collaborative data, and `/transformation_catalog/{transformationId}` for globally accessible read-only data, rules can be written more simply without acting as filters. For instance, to list pipelines a user has access to, the client queries `/workspaces/{workspaceId}/pipelines` where `workspaceId` is one the user is a member of. Security rules then verify `request.auth.uid` against the denormalized `workspaceMembers` map (or `workspaceOwnerId`) directly on the `pipeline` document, ensuring all returned documents are authorized. The addition of `/demo_pipelines/{demoPipelineId}` further exemplifies structural segregation, providing public read-only access to specific demo content without mixing it with private production data.

User onboarding involves creating a `UserProfile` document in `/users/{userId}/profile` upon first login. This document includes a `preferences` field that stores UI settings, notably defaulting to `darkMode: true` as requested. The `role` field within `UserProfile` will dictate UI views and default permissions but is not used for direct authorization on other documents to maintain Authorization Independence; instead, `ownerId` and denormalized `members` maps are used for resource access control. The 'demo mode' button will link to the `/demo_pipelines` collection, which is explicitly structured for public read access.
